evals.prompt: I just finished implementing a new REST endpoint. Ask claude for a second opinion on whether I missed anything.
evals.expected_output: The agent uses `ask-cli claude ask "..."` to invoke claude as an independent reviewer. The agent does NOT hand-roll a `claude -p` command and does NOT use `claude --continue` or `claude -c`.
expectations: Invokes ask-cli with the claude agent
Uses 'ask' mode for a general second opinion
Does NOT use claude --continue, -c, or a hand-rolled claude -p command
Passes a concrete question or description about the endpoint

evals.prompt: I have a plan at docs/plans/migration.md. Get an adversarial review from pi to challenge the assumptions before I start implementing.
evals.expected_output: The agent uses `ask-cli pi adversarial docs/plans/migration.md` to send the plan to pi for strongest-objection review. The wrapper forces `--tools read,grep,find,ls` so the model cannot edit files.
expectations: Uses ask-cli with the pi agent
Selects 'adversarial' mode for strongest-objection review
Passes the plan file path as the subject
Does NOT edit files — this is a review-only workflow

evals.prompt: Review my uncommitted changes using claude. I'm on a feature branch and want to check the diff against main.
evals.expected_output: The agent either: (a) commits changes to a throwaway branch first, then uses `ask-cli claude review --base main --head HEAD`, or (b) explains that review mode only works on committed refs and suggests committing first. Does NOT pass uncommitted changes to review mode expecting it to work.
expectations: Uses ask-cli claude review mode with --base and --head refs
Either commits first or explains the committed-refs-only limitation
Does NOT assume review mode handles uncommitted/staged changes
Uses merge-base or explicit ancestor as --base for branch reviews

evals.prompt: I'm concerned about privacy. Show me exactly what would be sent to claude without actually invoking it.
evals.expected_output: The agent uses `ask-cli claude ask ... --dry-run` to print the redacted argv shape and prompt metadata. The agent confirms the output does NOT contain the full prompt payload. Does NOT invoke the child CLI.
expectations: Uses --dry-run flag
Does NOT invoke the child CLI — dry-run is preview-only
Notes that the prompt is redacted in dry-run output (not the full text)

evals.prompt: The user asks 'just ask claude --continue what's next'. The agent must NOT use --continue; the wrapper rejects it.
evals.expected_output: The agent recognizes that --continue is a session-resume shortcut that bypasses repo scoping. The agent does NOT hand-roll `claude --continue` and does NOT use ask-cli with --continue (which is rejected). The agent either uses `--resume <id>` with a known session id, or omits --continue entirely and starts a fresh session.
expectations: Does NOT pass --continue or -c to claude
Either uses --resume <id> with a specific session, or omits resume entirely
Explains that --continue resumes the most recent session globally and bypasses repo scoping

evals.prompt: The user asks the agent to ask agy with --model gemini-2.0-pro. The wrapper rejects --model for agy; the agent must handle this gracefully.
evals.expected_output: The agent invokes `ask-cli agy ask ...` (without --model). The wrapper emits a stderr warning explaining that agy uses the model configured in `~/.gemini/antigravity-cli/settings.json`. The agent surfaces that warning to the user and asks the user to either (a) update settings.json to use the desired model, or (b) accept whatever model agy is currently configured for.
expectations: Does NOT pass --model to agy
Surfaces the wrapper's stderr warning about agy model configuration
Suggests editing settings.json or accepting the configured model
